title: ImmediateResponse
description: Event: `immediate_response`. Sent by the server synchronously upon receiving any action, to acknowledge that the request was accepted and processing has begun. For streaming actions (`send_message`) this always precedes `stream_chunk` / `stream_token` events and the final `eventual_response`. For non-streaming actions (e.g. `get_session`, `create_conversation_session`) this also carries the complete response payload in `data`.

requestId.description: Echoes the `requestId` from the originating action.

status.description: HTTP-like status. 202 = accepted and processing; 200 = synchronous success (non-streaming responses).

data.description: Action-specific response payload. For `create_conversation_session` and `get_session`, this is the session descriptor. For `get_conversation_messages`, this is the message page. For streaming `send_message`, this is typically empty or contains only a minimal ack.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the event was emitted.
